mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term assets combined with falling inflation indicates excess liquidity and weakening price pressures, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during expansion phases, with a directional hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below the 1.2 million unit threshold signals a collapse in construction demand and related supply chains, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 70% of periods where housing starts breach the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside stagnant or rising federal funds rates indicates real rate compression and monetary policy lag, which fuels equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal yields negative forward returns in more than 65% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3% confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during recovery phases.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3% fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery periods, with a directional accuracy below 50%.
